fields.name: Worksheet Template Name
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the name of the planning worksheet template.
The program automatically retrieves the name from the Name field in the Planning Worksheet Template table, but you can change it.

fields.name: Worksheet Batch Name
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the name of the planning worksheet batch.
The program automatically retrieves the name from the Name field in the Planning Worksheet Names window, but you can change it.

fields.name: Worksheet Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the line number of the requisition worksheet.
The program automatically retrieves the line number from the Line No. field in the Requisition Line table, but you can change it.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number for this planning worksheet line.
The program fills in the field automatically and uses the number for tracking purposes.

fields.name: Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item number of the component.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the description of the component.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity of the component.
The program automatically fills in the quantity from the Quantity field on the Production BOM Line table, but you can change it.

fields.name: Position
fields.tooltip: Specifies the position of the component on the bill of material.

fields.name: Position 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies the second reference number for the component position, such as the alternate position number of a component on a circuit board.

fields.name: Position 3
fields.tooltip: Specifies the third reference number for the component position on a bill of material, such as the alternate position number of a component on a print card.

fields.name: Lead-Time Offset
fields.tooltip: Specifies the lead-time offset for the planning component.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Expected Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the expected quantity of this planning component line.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies additional description text.

fields.name: Flushing Method
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the flushing method.
The value is copied from the Flushing Method field on the production order component line.
For more information, see the Flushing Method field on the tem card.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the inventory location, where the item on the planning component line will be registered.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a bin code for the item. click the field to see a list of available Bin codes for the location code.

fields.name: Supplied-by Line No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. It shows the production order line number that supplies the component line.

fields.name: Planning Level Code
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. The field indicates which planning level the item on the production order line belongs to. The program automatically fills in this field when it calculates a plan.

fields.name: Ref. Order Status
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the status of the production order. The field has the following four options:

Simulated


Planned


Firm Planned


Released


If the field is blank, this indicates a purchase order.

fields.name: Ref. Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the relevant production or purchase order.

fields.name: Ref. Order Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the relevant production order line number. The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Length
fields.tooltip: Specifies the length of one item unit when measured in the specified unit of measure.

fields.name: Width
fields.tooltip: Specifies the width of one item unit when measured in the specified unit of measure.

fields.name: Weight
fields.tooltip: Specifies the weight of one item unit when measured in the specified unit of measure.

fields.name: Depth
fields.tooltip: Specifies the depth of one item unit when measured in the specified unit of measure.

fields.name: Calculation Formula
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to calculate the Quantity field.

fields.name: Quantity per
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the component are required to produce the parent item.

fields.name: Ref. Order Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the order on the planning line is a purchase order, production order, transfer order, or assembly order.

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: Cost Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total cost for this planning component line.

fields.name: Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when this planning component must be finished.

fields.name: Due Time
fields.tooltip: Copies the time from the Starting Time field on the planning worksheet line to which the component belongs. This is the time that the component is needed.

fields.name: Direct Unit Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the direct unit costs for this planning component line.
The program automatically retrieves the unit cost from the item card, when the No. field is filled in, but you can change it.

fields.name: Indirect Cost %
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the indirect cost percentage.
The program automatically retrieves the indirect cost percentage from the Item table when the No. field is filled in, but you can change it.
You can identify an indirect cost percentage as part of the purchased item standard cost. An indirect cost can represent freight costs associated with the purchased item. It can also represent inventory carrying costs. The percentage is added to the direct cost of the item. Indirect cost is included in the calculation of the Standard Cost field on the item card.

fields.name: Overhead Rate
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the overhead rate.
The program automatically retrieves the overhead rate from the Item table when the No. field is filled in, but you can change it.
You can identify an overhead rate as part of the purchased item cost. An overhead rate can represent transport costs associated with the purchased item. It can also represent inventory carrying costs. The amount is added to the direct cost of the item. The overhead rate is included in the calculation of the Standard Cost field on the item card.

fields.name: Direct Cost Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the direct cost amount.
The program calculates the direct cost amount as direct unit cost multiplied by Expected Quantity.

fields.name: Overhead Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the overhead amount.
The program calculates the overhead amount as follows: (direct cost amount * indirect cost % + overhead rate) * expected quantity.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity per unit of measure.
If you have filled in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item card and entered a quantity for the selected item in the Qty. per Unit of Measure field, the program automatically fills in this field when you enter the item number on the line.

fields.name: Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the value in the Quantity field on the line.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reserved quantity of the item, in base units of measure.

fields.name: Reserved Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of units that are reserved.

fields.name: Expected Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the contents of the Expected Quantity field on the line, in base units of measure.

fields.name: Original Expected Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Shows the quantity, in base units of measure, which is stated on the existing production or purchase order when an action message proposes to change the quantity on an order.

fields.name: Net Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity, in the base unit of measure, that the planning component line contributes to the item's availability.
The program automatically calculates the field as the difference between the Original Quantity and Quantity fields multiplied by the quantity per unit of measure.

fields.name: Due Date-Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the due date and the due time, which are combined in a format called "due date-time".

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Critical
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if the parent item on the planning line is included in availability calculations to promise a shipment date.
This field does not influence the order promising functionality if the item itself is to be sold. It only affects items that are components of the item to be sold.

fields.name: Planning Line Origin
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the source of the planning line. If you manually enter the line, the field is blank.
The program uses this field internally.
